symbols.name: Command
symbols.purpose: Discriminated union type representing all slash commands; the three variants differ in execution model (prompt expansion, local function, or local JSX rendering).

symbols.name: CommandBase
symbols.signature: type CommandBase = { name, description, aliases?, isEnabled?, isHidden?, availability?, loadedFrom?, immediate?, ... }
symbols.purpose: Shared fields for every command regardless of type, including availability gates, feature-flag enablement, and source annotation.

symbols.name: PromptCommand
symbols.signature: type PromptCommand = { type: 'prompt'; getPromptForCommand(args, context): Promise<ContentBlockParam[]>; source; allowedTools?; model?; context?; agent?; paths?; ... }
symbols.purpose: Command variant that expands to an LLM prompt — covers skills, /commit, /review, and all markdown-defined commands.

symbols.name: LocalJSXCommand
symbols.purpose: Lazy-loaded Ink/React UI command; covers config panels, model picker, MCP manager, plan mode, etc.

symbols.name: LocalCommand
symbols.signature: type LocalCommand = { type: 'local'; supportsNonInteractive: boolean; load(): Promise<LocalCommandModule> }
symbols.purpose: Text-returning command that runs synchronously without UI; used for /compact, /version, and bridge-safe utilities.

symbols.name: CommandAvailability
symbols.purpose: Static auth-tier gate that hides commands from Bedrock/Vertex/custom-proxy users at the availability level before feature flags are consulted.

symbols.name: COMMANDS
symbols.purpose: Memoized factory that assembles the ~60 always-available builtin commands, conditionally appending feature-flagged and USER_TYPE=ant internal commands.

symbols.name: getCommands
symbols.purpose: Primary public API — merges all command sources (bundled skills, builtin plugins, skill dirs, workflows, plugin commands, builtins), then filters by availability and isEnabled; also splices in dynamically discovered skills.

symbols.name: loadAllCommands
symbols.purpose: Memoized (by cwd) loader that fans out to all five command sources in parallel and concatenates them in precedence order.

symbols.name: meetsAvailabilityRequirement
symbols.purpose: Filters commands by auth/provider tier without caching so /login changes take effect immediately.

symbols.name: getSkillToolCommands
symbols.purpose: Returns prompt-type commands that the model (SkillTool) can invoke — all skills plus legacy commands/ entries that have descriptions.

symbols.name: getSlashCommandToolSkills
symbols.purpose: Returns only skill-sourced prompt commands (skills/, plugin, bundled, or disableModelInvocation=true) for the slash-command tool context.

symbols.name: REMOTE_SAFE_COMMANDS
symbols.purpose: Explicit allowlist of commands safe in --remote mode (no local filesystem or IDE dependency); used to pre-filter before CCR init completes.

symbols.name: BRIDGE_SAFE_COMMANDS
symbols.purpose: Commands safe to execute when received over the Remote Control bridge from mobile/web clients — excludes local-jsx (Ink UI) commands.

symbols.name: INTERNAL_ONLY_COMMANDS
symbols.purpose: Commands compiled into the binary but exposed only when USER_TYPE=ant — backfill, bughunter, commit-push-pr, teleport, etc.

symbols.name: clearCommandsCache
symbols.purpose: Clears all memoization caches for commands, skill dirs, plugins, and the external skill index so /reload-plugins and dynamic skill discovery take effect.

symbols.name: findCommand
symbols.signature: function findCommand(commandName: string, commands: Command[]): Command | undefined
symbols.purpose: Resolves a slash-command name against name, userFacingName(), and aliases array.

symbols.name: formatDescriptionWithSource
symbols.purpose: Annotates a command description with its origin (bundled, plugin name, workflow, user setting path) for typeahead and help UI.

symbols.name: BundledSkillDefinition
symbols.signature: type BundledSkillDefinition = { name, description, aliases?, whenToUse?, allowedTools?, model?, files?, hooks?, context?, agent?, isEnabled?, getPromptForCommand }
symbols.purpose: Definition object passed to registerBundledSkill(); supports embedded reference files that are lazily extracted to a secure per-process temp directory on first invocation.

symbols.name: registerBundledSkill
symbols.purpose: Converts a BundledSkillDefinition into a Command and pushes it onto the module-level bundledSkills array; wraps getPromptForCommand to lazily extract embedded files on first call.

symbols.name: getBundledSkills
symbols.purpose: Returns a defensive copy of all registered bundled skills for inclusion in the command list.

symbols.name: initBundledSkills
symbols.purpose: Startup registration point that calls every individual register*Skill() function; feature-flagged skills (dream, hunter, loop, claudeApi, etc.) are conditionally required to eliminate dead code.

symbols.name: getSkillDirCommands
symbols.purpose: Loads skills from managed, user, project, and --add-dir /skills/ directories in parallel, deduplicates by resolved symlink path, then separates conditional (paths-gated) skills into a lazy activation store.

symbols.name: createSkillCommand
symbols.signature: function createSkillCommand({ skillName, markdownContent, source, baseDir, loadedFrom, ... }): Command
symbols.purpose: Constructs a PromptCommand from parsed markdown frontmatter; getPromptForCommand performs argument substitution, ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID} interpolation, and shell injection execution (blocked for MCP sources).

symbols.name: parseSkillFrontmatterFields
symbols.signature: export function parseSkillFrontmatterFields(frontmatter, markdownContent, resolvedName, descriptionFallbackLabel): { description, allowedTools, model, effort, hooks, executionContext, ... }
symbols.purpose: Centralised frontmatter parser used by both disk-based and MCP skill loaders to avoid duplication.

symbols.name: discoverSkillDirsForPaths
symbols.signature: async function discoverSkillDirsForPaths(filePaths: string[], cwd: string): Promise<string[]>
symbols.purpose: Called after file operations to walk up from touched paths looking for nested .claude/skills/ directories; gitignored directories are skipped as a security measure.

symbols.name: activateConditionalSkillsForPaths
symbols.purpose: Promotes path-filtered skills (those with paths frontmatter) into the dynamic skill map when the model touches a matching file, using gitignore-style pattern matching.

symbols.name: MCPSkillBuilders
symbols.signature: type MCPSkillBuilders = { createSkillCommand: typeof createSkillCommand; parseSkillFrontmatterFields: typeof parseSkillFrontmatterFields }
symbols.purpose: Write-once registry breaking the mcpSkills.ts → loadSkillsDir.ts import cycle; loadSkillsDir.ts writes at module init, mcpSkills.ts reads when an MCP server connects.

symbols.name: registerBuiltinPlugin
symbols.purpose: Inserts a user-toggleable built-in plugin into the BUILTIN_PLUGINS Map; plugins appear in /plugin UI and persist enabled state to user settings.

symbols.name: getBuiltinPluginSkillCommands
symbols.purpose: Returns Command objects from skills of all currently enabled built-in plugins.

symbols.name: initBuiltinPlugins
symbols.purpose: Startup hook for registering user-togglable built-in plugins; currently empty scaffolding for future migration of bundled skills that need /plugin-UI visibility.

symbols.name: CommandSpec
symbols.signature: type CommandSpec = { name, description?, subcommands?, args?, options? }
symbols.purpose: Lightweight descriptor for bash command completion specs (a subset of the Fig autocomplete format) used to provide argument hints for wrapped commands like timeout and srun.

symbols.name: getCommandSpec
symbols.signature: const getCommandSpec = memoizeWithLRU(async (command: string): Promise<CommandSpec | null>)
symbols.purpose: Looks up a bash command's argument spec first in the inline specs array, then falls back to @withfig/autocomplete dynamic import.

symbols.name: getPluginCommands
symbols.purpose: Loads markdown-defined commands from all enabled marketplace plugins, namespacing them as plugin:command and wiring up ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} variable substitution in allowed-tools.

symbols.name: getPluginSkills
symbols.purpose: Loads skills from enabled plugins' skills/ directories (or skillsPaths array from manifest), namespaced as plugin:skill.

symbols.name: createMovedToPluginCommand
symbols.signature: function createMovedToPluginCommand({ name, pluginName, pluginCommand, getPromptWhileMarketplaceIsPrivate }): Command
symbols.purpose: Factory for commands that have been migrated to the marketplace plugin ecosystem; ANT employees get a redirect message, external users get the legacy prompt during the private marketplace period.

data_flows.data: BundledSkillDefinition objects for each bundled skill
Snapshot array of all registered bundled skill Commands
Deduplicated array of disk-based skill Commands from managed/user/project dirs
Marketplace plugin Command objects namespaced as plugin:command
Filtered Command[] visible to current user in current cwd
Skills discovered in nested .claude/skills/ dirs after model touches files
Dynamically discovered Commands inserted before builtin commands
Conditional skills promoted when model touches matching file paths
Markdown text with substituted args, base-dir prefix, and executed shell snippets
References to createSkillCommand and parseSkillFrontmatterFields functions
Skill definitions from MCP server prompts converted to Commands with loadedFrom='mcp'
BundledSkillDefinitions from enabled built-in plugins converted to Commands
{ call } function for local commands or React node factory for JSX commands

patterns.name: Five-source command aggregation with precedence ordering
patterns.description: getCommands() assembles commands from five distinct sources in a fixed priority order: bundledSkills → builtinPluginSkills → skillDirCommands → workflowCommands → pluginCommands → COMMANDS() builtins. This ordering determines which source 'wins' when names collide. The assembly is memoized by cwd but the availability/isEnabled filter runs on every call to handle auth state changes mid-session.

patterns.name: Discriminated union command type with lazy load for UI commands
patterns.description: Commands are a three-way discriminated union on `type`: 'prompt' commands expand to LLM message content, 'local' commands return text synchronously, and 'local-jsx' commands render Ink/React UI. The two non-prompt variants use a `load()` function for lazy dynamic import, deferring heavy UI dependencies (Ink, complex parsers) until the command is actually invoked. This pattern is pervasive across ~50 JSX commands.

patterns.name: Bundled skills use embedded file extraction with security hardening
patterns.description: Skills with a `files` map in BundledSkillDefinition lazily extract those files to a per-process nonce directory (getBundledSkillsRoot()) on first invocation using O_CREAT|O_EXCL|O_NOFOLLOW to prevent symlink attacks. The extraction is promise-memoized so concurrent first calls await the same extraction rather than racing. The skill then prepends a 'Base directory for this skill: <dir>' line to the prompt so the model can Read/Grep reference files on demand.

patterns.name: Dynamic skill discovery triggered by file operations
patterns.description: When the model touches files (Read/Write/Edit/Bash), a hook walks up from those file paths looking for .claude/skills/ directories below cwd that weren't loaded at startup. Discovered skills are merged into a module-level Map, a signal fires to clear memoization caches, and getCommands() splices them in just before the builtin block. This means available slash commands can grow during a session as the model explores a codebase.

patterns.name: Conditional path-gated skills activated on file touch
patterns.description: Skills with a `paths` frontmatter field (gitignore-style patterns) are separated from unconditional skills at load time and held in a conditionalSkills Map. activateConditionalSkillsForPaths() runs on every file operation and promotes matching skills into the dynamic skills map using the `ignore` library for pattern matching — the same semantics as CLAUDE.md conditional rules.

patterns.name: MCPSkillBuilders registry as a cycle-breaking injection point
patterns.description: To avoid a circular import between mcpSkills.ts and loadSkillsDir.ts (both of which transitively reach client.ts), mcpSkillBuilders.ts acts as a write-once registry. loadSkillsDir.ts registers the two builder functions as a side-effect of module initialization (which happens eagerly via commands.ts static import), and mcpSkills.ts reads them later when an MCP server connects. This is explicitly documented as an alternative to non-literal dynamic imports, which fail in Bun bundled binaries.

patterns.name: Feature-flag dead-code elimination via bun:bundle feature()
patterns.description: Feature-flagged commands and skills use conditional require() wrapped in feature('FLAG') checks. The Bun bundler evaluates these at build time and eliminates dead branches, so unreleased features (KAIROS, VOICE_MODE, BRIDGE_MODE, ULTRAPLAN, etc.) are absent from the production binary. The same pattern applies in initBundledSkills() for skill registration.

patterns.name: Built-in plugins as user-toggleable wrappers over bundled skill definitions
patterns.description: builtinPlugins.ts defines a parallel plugin concept distinct from both marketplace plugins and bundled skills. Built-in plugins appear in the /plugin UI and persist enabled state to user settings, but their skill definitions reuse BundledSkillDefinition and are converted to Commands via skillDefinitionToCommand() with source='bundled' (not 'builtin') to keep them visible in the Skill tool listing. The actual registry is currently empty — it is scaffolding for migrating bundled skills that need user-toggling.

patterns.name: Bash specs as a Fig-compatible argument completion registry
patterns.description: utils/bash/specs/ contains a small set of handwritten CommandSpec objects (timeout, srun, alias, pyright, sleep, nohup, time) that mirror the Fig/autocomplete schema. getCommandSpec() tries these first, then falls back to @withfig/autocomplete dynamic imports for the long tail of standard Unix commands. This provides shell argument completion hints without bundling the full Fig spec library.

surprises: Bundled skills can embed reference files (via BundledSkillDefinition.files) that are extracted to a nonce temp dir with O_EXCL|O_NOFOLLOW on first invocation — full symlink-attack hardening inside a skill framework
Available slash commands can expand mid-session: file operations trigger upward directory walks that discover and register new .claude/skills/ dirs below cwd, then splice them into the command list

Path-gated skills (paths frontmatter) remain dormant until the model touches a matching file, then activate permanently for the rest of the session — skills can become visible as a side effect of reading files

The mcpSkillBuilders.ts write-once registry exists solely to break an import cycle that would cause dynamic imports to fail in Bun-bundled binaries — explicit documentation of a runtime constraint driving architecture
Built-in plugins (plugins/builtinPlugins.ts) form a third, currently empty tier between bundled skills and marketplace plugins — the registry exists with full infrastructure but no registrations yet

MCP skills are blocked from executing inline shell commands (!`...` / ```!...```) in their markdown bodies — the only security distinction between loadedFrom='mcp' vs other sources in createSkillCommand
INTERNAL_ONLY_COMMANDS are compiled into the binary for all users but exposed only when USER_TYPE=ant; external users running a dev build get no error — the commands simply never appear

The /review and /ultrareview commands are split: /review is a pure local prompt command, /ultrareview is a local-jsx command that gates on isUltrareviewEnabled() and can trigger a remote CCR execution path
Bash command completion specs mirror the Fig autocomplete schema but only 7 custom specs are bundled inline — the rest are lazy-loaded from @withfig/autocomplete using dynamic import(command)

The createMovedToPluginCommand factory contains a dual-path implementation: ANT users get a plugin migration message, while external users get the legacy prompt — a migration shim preserving backward compatibility during a private marketplace rollout
